title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Hand dataset_viewer() data frame. entire API common case — everything else interaction inside widget. Try : drag scrollbar, drag column border resize, click row. grid live — real widget, screenshot.

what: A guided tour of the interface
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: layout mirrors SAS Studio, anyone used viewer already home: Columns panel (left) — checklist every column type chip (character, # numeric, calendar dates). Uncheck column hide grid; data never reloaded. Sort list original order, name, type, filter name find columns wide dataset (list order navigation aid; grid column order unchanged). Property pane (lower left) — select column inspect Label, Name, Length, Type, Format, attributes PROC CONTENTS reports. Toolbar (top) — names-versus-labels View dropdown, Export current view CSV button, Show code button (<>) reveals dplyr pipeline current view, Filter table rows (funnel) badge showing active filter. Header sort — click column header select , click cycle sort: ascending, descending, back unsorted. Shift-click headers build multi-column sort; sorted column shows direction priority (AGE ↑1, SEX ↓2). Header menu — right-click column header sort (Sort Ascending / Descending add column sort; Clear Sorting removes just column), add filter, copy column header, size columns content. Status bar — total row column counts, filtered count filter active. Tip Sorting filtering driven widget, R arguments, reader report can explore data without re-running code.

what: CDISC metadata, labels, and the property pane
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: plain data frame labels, property pane shows names . Point viewer labelled CDISC-conformed frame metadata comes life. companion artoo package installed, column labels, formats, storage lengths read straight frame shown property pane — can set header row use labels instead names. Select STUDYID columns panel property pane reads Study Identifier; header row now shows labels view = "labels". artoo dependency required plain frames — consulted present. Note dataset_viewer() also accepts path dataset file (dataset_viewer("adsl.parquet")); artoo::read_dataset() reads , carrying metadata property pane.
code: # artoo ships the CDISC pilot ADaM datasets used across these docs. dataset_viewer(artoo::cdisc_adsl, view = "labels")

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: two ways filter, operating every row: Filter Table Rows — click funnel toolbar type free-text expression, SAS-style, AGE >= 75 SEX = "F". translated SQL clause, status bar updates matched count. Add Filter — right-click column header. dialog adapts column’s type: checklist distinct values character columns, comparison operator value numbers, date picker dates. filter runs DuckDB full Parquet payload, answer exact — matched count true count, count within sampled window.

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Exploration grid convenient, report needs reproducible. Show code button (<> toolbar) opens dialog runnable dplyr pipeline reproduces current view — filter, sort, column selection, order: select() comes last filter sort can reference column view hides — narrowing first drop steps run. snippet air-formatted syntax-highlighted, Copy button. SQL idioms translated R equivalents — (...) becomes %% c(...), becomes !x %% c(...), date time literals become .Date() / .POSIXct() / hms::as_hms() — code runs -source frame. modelled SAS Studio’s “show code creates table”, stays sync view: change filter sort reopen see updated pipeline.
code: library(dplyr)  mtcars |>   filter(mpg >= 20) |>   arrange(desc(hp)) |>   select(cyl, hp, wt, mpg)

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Export current view CSV toolbar button downloads exactly looking — visible columns, active filter, current sort, every matching row, just visible window. export streams engine row chunks, depend dataset fitting memory one piece.

what: Built for scale: no row sampling
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: design choice makes work moving query engine browser: Transport. frame serialised Parquet nanoparquet carried widget payload — columnar, compressed, read natively engine. Engine. DuckDB-WASM reads Parquet directly answers every filter, sort, page SQL. Grid. canvas grid asks engine rows visible window (LIMIT/OFFSET query), scrolling cost independent dataset’s size. practical upshot: viewer multi-million-row frame scrolls, sorts, filters smoothly one mtcars, every row stays reachable.

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Shiny, pair datasetviewerOutput() UI renderDatasetViewer() server. viewer dead end: user’s current column selection, filter, sort, view mode flow back app inputs, namespaced output id, rest app can react analyst looking .
code: library(shiny) library(datasetviewer)  ui <- fluidPage(   datasetviewerOutput("viewer", height = "560px"),   verbatimTextOutput("state") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$viewer <- renderDatasetViewer(dataset_viewer(mtcars))    # State changes in the widget arrive as inputs, namespaced by output id.   output$state <- renderPrint({     list(       columns = input$viewer_columns, # columns currently shown       filter  = input$viewer_filter, # active filter expression       sort    = input$viewer_sort, # active sort       view    = input$viewer_view # "names" or "labels"     )   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: server required static case — vignette embeds live widgets. Drop dataset_viewer() R Markdown Quarto document result fully interactive grid. call write Shiny app produces viewer .

what: The query engine: online by default, offline when you need it
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: Everything except one piece bundled package works internet: canvas grid, column panel, filters, code view, CSV export, data (carried page Parquet). one piece DuckDB-WASM query engine — -browser database answers every filter, sort, page. roughly 35 MB, far large ship inside R package, default widget loads public CDN (jsDelivr) first time grid rendered. interactive use connected machine, nothing needed. browser reach CDN — air-gapped laptop, corporate Shiny server behind firewall — engine must served locally. works way arrow package acquires C++ library: install time, function call. install datasetviewer, install step fetches engine (parquet extension DuckDB needs read payload) package. , Shiny app serves engine package browser — internet runtime. install machine reach public host, step skipped widget simply falls back CDN; install never fails. fetch steered environment variables, set install.packages("datasetviewer") (analogues arrow’s LIBARROW_BINARY): typical corporate deployment installs package way installs (often internal mirror already carries arrow), points variables -house mirror public host blocked, runs Shiny app — serves engine every user’s browser outbound connection. self-contained HTML document (Quarto R Markdown report, htmlwidgets::saveWidget(selfcontained = TRUE)) embeds every dependency file. Embedding 35 MB engine produce enormous page, static documents load engine CDN instead. Set option , setup chunk, dataset_viewer() call: keeps document small grid loads engine CDN reader opens . (vignette exactly .) Leave option default Shiny, engine served rather embedded.

what: Self-hosting the engine for offline and corporate use
title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: browser reach CDN — air-gapped laptop, corporate Shiny server behind firewall — engine must served locally. works way arrow package acquires C++ library: install time, function call. install datasetviewer, install step fetches engine (parquet extension DuckDB needs read payload) package. , Shiny app serves engine package browser — internet runtime. install machine reach public host, step skipped widget simply falls back CDN; install never fails. fetch steered environment variables, set install.packages("datasetviewer") (analogues arrow’s LIBARROW_BINARY): typical corporate deployment installs package way installs (often internal mirror already carries arrow), points variables -house mirror public host blocked, runs Shiny app — serves engine every user’s browser outbound connection.

title: Get started with datasetviewer
text: self-contained HTML document (Quarto R Markdown report, htmlwidgets::saveWidget(selfcontained = TRUE)) embeds every dependency file. Embedding 35 MB engine produce enormous page, static documents load engine CDN instead. Set option , setup chunk, dataset_viewer() call: keeps document small grid loads engine CDN reader opens . (vignette exactly .) Leave option default Shiny, engine served rather embedded.

what: Your first look at the interface
title: Understanding the interface
text: window divides left rail (column tools metadata), data grid, thin status bar — SAS Studio layout.  Toolbar — names-versus-labels View dropdown, Export, Show code, Filter, row pager. Columns panel — checklist every column, type chips tools sort search list. Property pane — PROC CONTENTS-style metadata selected column. Data grid — canvas grid draws rows can see, stays fast size. Status bar — total row column counts, filtered count filter active.

title: Understanding the interface
text: View — switch header row column names column labels. Export current view CSV — download visible columns, current filter, sort, every matching row. Show code — reveal runnable dplyr pipeline current view. Filter table rows — open free-text filter; badge shows active expression. Row pager — first / previous / next / last, visible row range.

title: Understanding the interface
text: Use panel show hide columns, navigate wide dataset, pick column whose metadata property pane shows.  Collapse — hide whole rail widen grid (handle reopens ). Filter — type narrow list column name label; navigation aid , never changes grid’s column order. Sort list — order list original position, name, type. Select — show hide every column . Column row — checkbox, type chip, name label. Uncheck hide column grid (data never reloaded); click row select property pane. type chip names column’s storage kind glance:

title: Understanding the interface
text: Selecting column panel fills property pane attributes PROC CONTENTS reports.  plain data frame labels shows names ; point viewer labelled CDISC-conformed frame pane comes life.

title: Understanding the interface
text: View dropdown swaps header row variable names labels — useful clinical data, names terse codes labels human-readable description.  Column labels  Column names Start viewer either mode view argument (dataset_viewer(x, view = "labels")).

title: Understanding the interface
text: Click column header select , click cycle sort: ascending → descending → unsorted. Shift-click headers build multi-column sort; sorted column shows direction priority.  primary key, ascending — AGE ↑1. secondary key, descending — SEX ↓2. Rows ordered age, ties broken sex. Tip multi-column sort, Shift-click additional header. little number beside arrow (↑1, ↓2) sort priority, can always read sort order straight headers.

title: Understanding the interface
text: Right-click column header per-column menu.

title: Understanding the interface
text: two ways filter, evaluated DuckDB every row, matched count status bar exact — never count within sampled window. funnel toolbar opens free-text box. Type SAS-style expression; becomes SQL clause.  Expression — filter, AGE >= 70 SEX = "F". Help (?) — syntax reference. Clear Filter — remove active filter without closing. Apply — validate apply; invalid expression reports inline. grammar small: Add Filter header menu opens dialog tailored column’s type. character (logical) column offers checklist distinct values:  prompt — select one values. distinct values, checkbox (plus (Missing) entry column NA). numeric column offers comparison operators value, + add conditions:  comparison operator (=, ≥, >, ≤, <). value compare . + — add another condition. date, date-time, time column offers native pickers equal / less-/ greater-:  criterion (Equal , Less , Greater ). native date picker — date-time time columns show matching native control. Note Every filter — free-text type-aware — runs full dataset browser, status bar’s Filtered rows count true count.

title: Understanding the interface
text: missing value renders muted NA every column, keeping distinct empty string floating-point NaN (renders NaN).  discontinuation flag, muted NA sits among real Y values. Filter missing values na / na free-text predicate (Missing) option Add Filter dialog; generated dplyr code uses .na().

Show code button opens runnable, air-formatted dplyr pipeline reproduces current view.  pipeline: filter(), arrange(), select() — matching filter, sort, column selection currently grid. Copy puts clipboard. Important select() comes last filter sort can reference column view hides — narrowing first drop column steps run. SQL idioms translated R equivalents ((…) becomes %% c(…), date time literals become .Date() / .POSIXct() / hms::as_hms()), snippet runs -source frame.

what: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: interactive, SAS Studio-style dataset viewer R. datasetviewer renders dataset fast, scrollable grid modelled SAS Studio table viewer, one htmlwidget runs interactive Shiny apps static HTML documents. Fast large data, sampling. data sent browser Parquet queried place DuckDB-WASM, filtering, sorting, scrolling stay responsive datasets overwhelm DOM-bound grids server-paged tables. SAS Studio layout. column-selection panel char/num type icons collapse toggle, property pane (Label, Name, Length, Type, Format), names-versus-labels header toggle, click--header sort (Shift-click multi-column sort), free-text “Filter Table Rows” expression. Clinical metadata. sibling artoo package installed, column labels, formats, storage lengths read labelled CDISC-conformed frame. Plain data frames work zero artoo dependency. Runs offline / behind firewall. grid, filters, code view, CSV export bundled package. DuckDB-WASM engine loads CDN default fetched package install time, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime.

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: Install released version CRAN: development version GitHub:

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: Click column header select , click cycle sort — ascending, descending, back unsorted. Shift-click additional headers build multi-column sort, shown direction priority (AGE ↑1, SEX ↓2). right-click menu, Sort Ascending/Descending add column sort Clear Sorting removes just column; can also add filter copy column header. Click funnel toolbar edit filter expression directly (example AGE > 50 SEX = "M").
code: library(datasetviewer)  # a plain data frame dataset_viewer(mtcars)  # an artoo-conformed frame, headers shown as labels adsl <- artoo::read_dataset("adsl.parquet") dataset_viewer(adsl, view = "labels")

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
code: library(shiny) ui <- fluidPage(datasetviewerOutput("viewer", height = "500px")) server <- function(input, output, session) {   output$viewer <- renderDatasetViewer(dataset_viewer(adsl))   # input$viewer_filter, input$viewer_sort, input$viewer_columns,   # input$viewer_view track the user's current view } shinyApp(ui, server)

title: SAS Studio-style dataset viewer for R
text: Everything except DuckDB-WASM query engine (~35 MB, large ship package) bundled works offline. engine loads CDN default, — like arrow package’s C++ library — fetched package install time, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime. Steer install-time fetch air-gapped mirrored environments: install never fails engine fetched; widget falls back CDN runtime. self-contained HTML documents, set options(datasetviewer.use_local_engine = FALSE) load engine CDN instead embedding . See vignette("datasetviewer") details.

what: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: Renders fast, scrollable data grid column-selection panel per-column property metadata. widget renders interactive Shiny apps static HTML documents.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
code: dataset_viewer(   x,   ...,   view = c("names", "labels"),   width = NULL,   height = NULL,   elementId = NULL )

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: x Dataset view. <data.frame | character(1)>. data frame, file path read via artoo::read_dataset() (xpt, Dataset-JSON, NDJSON, 'Parquet', RDS). artoo-conformed frame supplies labels, formats, lengths property panel; plain frame uses synthesized metadata. ... Reserved future arguments. view Initial header mode. <character(1)>. "names" (default, matching SAS Studio) shows column names headers; "labels" shows labels, falling back names label absent. width, height Widget sizing. <character(1) | numeric(1) | NULL>. Passed htmlwidgets::createWidget(). elementId Explicit DOM id. <character(1) | NULL>. Usually left NULL htmlwidgets assigns one.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: htmlwidget. Print render, use Shiny output.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
text: Query engine. data sent browser 'Parquet' queried place DuckDB-WASM, filter, sort, paging run whole dataset row sampling. engine (~35 MB) loads CDN default fetched package install time reachable, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime. Set options(datasetviewer.use_local_engine = FALSE) force CDN (small self-contained HTML). See vignette("datasetviewer") offline corporate deployment, including DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_* install-time environment variables.

title: View a dataset in an interactive SAS Studio-style grid — dataset_viewer
code: # ---- Example 1: view a plain data frame ---- # # Wrap any data frame to get the interactive grid. Printing the widget in # an interactive session or a rendered document shows it; here we inspect # the payload so the example stays headless and self-contained (printing a # widget would launch a browser under R CMD check). viewer <- dataset_viewer(mtcars) viewer$x$n_rows #> [1] 32  # ---- Example 2: CDISC labels as headers ---- # # With the sibling artoo package, a CDISC-conformed frame supplies column # labels, formats, and storage lengths to the property pane and the # names-versus-labels header toggle. Start on labels with view = "labels". if (requireNamespace("artoo", quietly = TRUE)) {   labelled <- dataset_viewer(artoo::cdisc_adsl, view = "labels")   labelled$x$columns[[1]]$label } #> [1] "Study Identifier"

what: datasetviewer: 'SAS Studio'-Style Interactive Dataset Viewer — datasetviewer-package
title: datasetviewer: 'SAS Studio'-Style Interactive Dataset Viewer — datasetviewer-package
text: interactive dataset viewer renders fast, scrollable grid column-selection panel, per-column property metadata, names-versus-labels header toggle, modelled 'SAS Studio' table viewer. Runs one codebase interactive 'Shiny' apps static HTML documents, free-text row filter, header sort, CSV export, handles large datasets without row sampling querying browser 'DuckDB-WASM'.

title: datasetviewer: 'SAS Studio'-Style Interactive Dataset Viewer — datasetviewer-package

what: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: Output render functions embed dataset_viewer() Shiny app. widget pushes live view state back Shiny inputs, app can reuse user's filter, sort, column selection server-side.

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
code: datasetviewerOutput(outputId, width = "100%", height = "500px")  renderDatasetViewer(expr, env = parent.frame(), quoted = FALSE)

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: outputId Output slot id. <character(1)>. Matched datasetviewerOutput() renderDatasetViewer(). width, height CSS sizing. <character(1)>. valid CSS size; grid fills element. expr Render expression. call returns dataset_viewer() widget. env, quoted Evaluation control. Standard htmlwidgets render plumbing; leave defaults.

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: datasetviewerOutput() returns Shiny output UI element; renderDatasetViewer() returns Shiny render function.

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
text: Inputs published. output id "viewer" widget sets, every change: input$viewer_columns (<character>): selected column names. input$viewer_filter (<character(1)>): filter expression. input$viewer_sort (<list>): sort keys (name, dir). input$viewer_view (<character(1)>): "names" "labels".

title: Shiny bindings for the dataset viewer — datasetviewer-shiny
code: # ---- Example 1: read the viewer's filter and selection server-side ---- # # The app shows a dataset and echoes the filter expression and selected # columns the user builds in the grid. Runs only in an interactive session. if (interactive()) {   library(shiny)   ui <- fluidPage(     datasetviewerOutput("viewer", height = "500px"),     verbatimTextOutput("state")   )   server <- function(input, output, session) {     output$viewer <- renderDatasetViewer(dataset_viewer(mtcars))     output$state <- renderText({       paste0(         "filter: ", input$viewer_filter, "\n",         "columns: ", paste(input$viewer_columns, collapse = ", ")       )     })   }   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: datasetviewer 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-09 Initial CRAN release. dataset_viewer() renders SAS Studio-style interactive grid data frame, artoo-conformed frame, file path read via artoo::read_dataset(). data sent browser Parquet queried place DuckDB-WASM, filter, sort, scroll stay fast large datasets without sampling rows. shell provides column-selection panel char/num type icons collapse chevron (list can sorted original order, name, type filtered name navigate wide datasets, without changing grid column order), property pane (Label, Name, Length, Type, Format), names-versus-labels header toggle, click--header sort (first click selects column, clicks cycle ascending, descending, unsorted) – Shift-click adds columns multi-column sort, shown direction priority. right-click menu sorts per column (Sort Ascending/Descending add column sort; Clear Sorting removes just column) offers per-column filters, copying column header, size--content, alongside free-text row filter CSV export current view. Missing values display muted NA every column (numeric, character, date), keeping distinct genuine empty string NaN floating-point value, renders NaN. can filtered COL na / COL na free-text predicate per-column “(Missing)” option Add Filter dialog; generated dplyr code uses .na(). datasetviewerOutput() renderDatasetViewer() embed widget Shiny publish current filter, sort, column selection, view mode inputs. “Show code” toolbar button emits runnable, air-formatted dplyr pipeline (filter, arrange, select, SQL--R translation) reproduces current view. DuckDB-WASM engine loads CDN default fetched package install time reachable, Shiny app can serve browsers internet runtime (offline / corporate deployment). install-time fetch honours DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_DIR, DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_URL, DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_EXT_URL, DATASETVIEWER_DUCKDB_OFFLINE; install never fails engine fetched (falls back CDN runtime). options(datasetviewer.use_local_engine = FALSE) forces CDN even engine present locally, keeping self-contained HTML documents small.
